summary:
Pulpotomy is performed to preserve the vital tissue of immature permanent teeth while reducing inflammation; it specifically targets diseased portions of the pulp to prevent further spread of infection. This procedure helps maintain the natural structure and function of the tooth during its development phase. \boxed{D}

The summary focuses on the essential goals of pulpotomy—preserving viability and preventing infection—in line with Agent2’s original response. It maintains clarity around both therapeutic intent and practical application within pedodontic practice.
